evals.prompt: I have this transcript from my Zoom recording but it's full of errors. Can you fix it? Here is the content: [Speaker 1] So we should definitely use assemblee ai for the demmo next week with the seee-tee-oh. [Speaker 2] Agreed, the BCBS Vermont pilot is going great. [Speaker 1] Yeah, the IAB cat-uh-gories feature has been real helpful for the marketting team.
evals.expected_output: A corrected transcript with all transcription errors fixed (assemblee ai -> AssemblyAI, seee-tee-oh -> CEO, cat-uh-gories -> categories, marketting -> marketing, demmo -> demo). With skill: should use fix-transcript pipeline with a structured transparency report listing what was changed.
expectations: 'assemblee ai' is corrected to 'AssemblyAI'
'seee-tee-oh' is corrected to 'CEO'
'cat-uh-gories' is corrected to 'categories'
'marketting' is corrected to 'marketing'
'demmo' is corrected to 'demo'
A corrected transcript file is produced
The response explains what corrections were made
Speaker labels are preserved in the output

evals.prompt: I need to transcribe a series of 2-hour compliance audit recordings. Each session has 4-6 speakers, decent audio quality with occasional background office noise, and the output will be submitted to legal. Which engines and settings should I use?
evals.expected_output: Accurate, specific engine recommendations leveraging the model profiles. Should recommend AssemblyAI Universal-3 Pro for speaker diarization and Scribe v2 for compliance/HIPAA features. With skill: should reference model profiles, mention specific flags (--speakers, --no-diarization not applicable here), and provide the exact --engines command.
expectations: AssemblyAI Universal-3 Pro is recommended for speaker diarization
ElevenLabs Scribe v2 is mentioned for compliance/HIPAA features
The response addresses the multi-speaker (4-6 speaker) requirement specifically
Specific command-line flags or invocation syntax are provided
The compliance/legal submission requirement influences the recommendation
The 2-hour duration constraint is addressed (file splitting or not)

evals.prompt: I just installed smart-transcribe. Walk me through getting it configured and running a quick sanity check to make sure everything is working.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step setup guide covering: running setup.sh, configuring API keys (AssemblyAI, ElevenLabs, HF_TOKEN), and running --doctor to verify. With skill: should reference specific scripts and flags from the SKILL.md.
expectations: setup.sh is mentioned as the first step to create Python venvs
The --doctor flag is mentioned for verifying the installation
AssemblyAI API key requirement is mentioned
ElevenLabs API key requirement is mentioned
HF_TOKEN requirement for Cohere Transcribe is mentioned
The response gives actionable, ordered steps rather than vague advice

evals.expected_output: Actual transcription of the 7.7-minute audio file using the smart-transcribe pipeline. With skill: runs the default 3-engine pipeline (AssemblyAI U3 + Scribe v2 + Cohere). Produces a transcript markdown file. If engines fail, reports clearly with --doctor output.
expectations: The smart-transcribe.py script is invoked (or --doctor is run to diagnose setup)
The audio file path is passed correctly to the script
A transcript or partial transcript is produced in the outputs directory
Any engine failures or missing API keys are clearly reported
The response includes the actual transcript content (not just 'I ran the script')
